metadata.title: Warranty Claim Audit Rules
metadata.purpose: Deterministic pre-submit and resubmit checks for Hyundai warranty-related claims using claim screen, repair order, VIS, STUI, and supporting documents.

field_resolution_rules.reason: STUI is the strongest attachment-side proof of the vehicle mileage tied to the final documentation event.
The claim should be built on the RO shown on the claim screen unless the audit finds that RO already claimed or the repair story requires original RO cross-reference.
Many warranty entry failures occur because the causal part family, replacement part family, and labor op family do not align.
Multiple STUI event dates may indicate a clean wait-for-parts timeline or a mixed attachment package that needs explanation.

hard_stop_rules.title: Missing VIN across primary documents
hard_stop_rules.description: A claim cannot be audited safely if the VIN cannot be identified from the uploaded or pasted materials.

hard_stop_rules.title: Causal part family mismatch
hard_stop_rules.description: The entered causal part does not align with the replacement part family or the labor op family.

hard_stop_rules.title: Final mileage mismatch against latest STUI
hard_stop_rules.description: The mileage entered on the claim does not match the latest STUI-captured mileage for the same VIN and repair event.

hard_stop_rules.title: Required STUI categories missing
hard_stop_rules.description: Required attachment categories are missing for the claim type.

hard_stop_rules.title: Current claim built on an earlier RO already shown in claim history
hard_stop_rules.description: VIS shows the same RO already claimed; the current claim should be corrected or moved to the correct RO before submission.

warning_rules.title: Multiple STUI attachment dates detected
warning_rules.description: STUI attachments appear to come from more than one repair event date. Confirm whether this is a clean wait-for-parts story or a mixed attachment package.

warning_rules.title: Campaign or recall language found in RO history while claim type is warranty
warning_rules.description: Campaign or recall language was found in the RO or VIS history. Confirm that the current issue is separate from campaign work.

warning_rules.title: Open campaigns found on VIS
warning_rules.description: VIS indicates open campaigns not yet performed. The current issue may need campaign separation review.

warning_rules.title: Wait-for-parts pattern detected
warning_rules.description: The dates and supporting records suggest an incomplete repair followed by a later completion. Confirm original presentation data and cross-reference the earlier RO.

warning_rules.title: Mileage differs between claim screen and repair order
warning_rules.description: The claim mileage and repair order mileage do not match. Confirm final mileage before submission.

warning_rules.title: No complaint/cause/correction detail found
warning_rules.description: The pasted or extracted claim screen text does not clearly show complaint, cause, and correction detail.

info_rules.title: Earlier related RO found in VIS history
info_rules.description: VIS shows earlier maintenance or claim history for the same VIN. This may simply be context, or it may matter for a wait-for-parts or repeat-visit story.

info_rules.title: Prior approval history not found on VIS
info_rules.description: No prior approval history was found on VIS. This is informational unless the repair type requires PA.
